title: Rebuild Series FAR Four Adams and The Chosen Children
display_title: Rebuild Series FAR Four Adams and The Chosen Children
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild Series: FAR, Four Adams & The Chosen Children             Quote Post by Asavarii » Sat Aug 16, 2014 9:39 am Introduction I recently watched 2.0 & 3.0 back-to-back when they came to a cinema near me (Melbourne, Australia). It was also the first time I had seen 3.0 with official subtitles. Then I got to wondering: during 2.0: during Second Impact, when four Adams appear along with four spears, what happened to them, and where did they go? Afterward, I got to thinking about the origins of the original Adam, the original Lance of Longinus and the White Moon, namely the First Ancestral Race (according to the classified information). I also got to thinking about Eva Unit-01 during the final scenes of End of Evangelion, floating off into space – a reminder that Humanity even existed, according to Yui. So what if the FAR are still active in the galaxy and discover Eva 01 floating out there...? The FAR’s involvement in the Rebuild Series I hypothesize that the FAR retrieved Eva 01, and Yui’s soul within it, and immediately recognised a synthesized life form bearing both the Fruits of Life and Wisdom. They therefore recognize that a race of their creation has succeeded in copying THEM. At this point their reaction might have been freak out, or perhaps they see in humanity a potential successor/benefactor (to their cause of seeding the galaxy with life). Either way, they need to learn more about what it is to be human. By examining Yui’s soul, the FAR realize that to determine whether humans “make the cut” they must understand the experience of what it is to be human, namely experience humanity in all of its sensory input: Sight, Sound, Touch, Smell and Taste. They also learn from Yui that the Earth has been wiped of human life, after Shinji rejects Instrumentality in EoE (with the exception of Shinji and Asuka, of course). In order to bring humans into the fold, the FAR send an Adam and Lilith back to Earth, along with four lances and an updated set of Dead Sea Scrolls (documenting how to gain ascendancy). The function of this Adam was to simultaneously generate Angels for humanity to fight with the Evas and observe the human sensory conditions of Sound, Touch, Smell and Taste, with the intention of fracturing his soul into four parts to experience life as a human, with each part dedicated to its sensory aspect. The function of Lilith was to recreate humanity into their previous forms, in preparation for their “covenant” with her, as well as observe humans visually. Instead of a Black Moon like the one Lilith originally came to Earth in, they send her in the grail-like object that is pulled from the earth in 3.0, where the Adams travel in either the AAA Wunder or another White Moon. In my opinion, the Wunder is the more plausible theory, as it begins 3.0 immobile and located in the Antarctic (more accurately polar region, not specified) where Second Impact occurred. It also seems large enough to bear four beings the size of the original Adam, and is even referred to as an Ark. The Four pieces of Adam Leading to and during Second Impact in the Rebuild continuity, The Katsuragi Expedition discovers the Wunder and Adam beneath Antarctica as depicted in the 2.0 flashback, Adam awakens from stasis and disperses himself into four parts, or Four Adams. This means there were four Adam soul fragments released. Adam’s physical remains shown so far is the Key of Nebuchadnezzar seen in 2.0, seemingly replacing Adam’s embryonic form from NGE. Where Adam’s embryonic form in NGE was a full life form, the Key in 2.0 merely resembles a human nervous system. For my theory to hold water there would need to be four other artefacts, one for each sensory input. The Four Vessels of Adam At the beginning of the Rebuild series, where do these Adam fragments reside? And where is Lilith in all this? Does her soul remain within her hibernating, crucified body in Terminal Dogma, or is it within a Rei clone, like in NGE? This is where the rules laid down by NGE concerning soul fragments inhabiting human vessels get a little hazy. One thing is for certain; a fractured soul can exist within either a naturally occurring human being (see Kyoko Zeppelin Soryu) or a cloned body (see Rei 1, 2 and 3, and possibly Kaworu). It is worth noting that where the soul of an Angel or Seed can exist within a cloned human vessel with little consequence (angelic powers, AT fields, etc), a naturally-born human being who is inhabited by a fractured soul is driven insane (eg. Kyoko). The exact process for the salvaging of a soul within a clone, like Kaworu or Rei in NGE, is never revealed. We can preclude that possibility however, especially if the process was detailed within the Dead Sea Scrolls retrieved by SEELE. Assuming this and in combination with the existence of fractured souls, let us also assume that it is possible to salvage a fraction of soul into a preoccupied vessel. So, we have two possible candidates for the vessel of a fragment of Adam’s soul; a clone or naturally occurring life-form. In the rebuild continuity, Shinji is the only pilot to have a confirmed mother, so he can be ruled out as a clone or vessel of Adam. Ironically, we are left with four convenient candidates, namely Asuka, Mari, Rei 2/Q and Kaworu. The Rei from 2.0 is effectively confirmed as a clone, as is Rei Q. Interestingly, Rei Q’s soul is said to be elsewhere by Kaworu, whereas Rei 2.0 seems to observe and make notes of visual cues from humans around her. Therefore, without compromising the possibility of a portion of Lilith’s soul and mission being housed within Rei Q, Rei 2.0 seems a good candidate for housing the soul of Lilith and her observational sight,. Kaworu awakens from a coffin on the moon and certainly displays angelic powers (breathing in space, for one). It’s not a stretch to imagine that Kaworu is also a clone, and seems highly pre-occupied with the human experience of sound (piano playing). Asuka and Mari are both natural births, given their abundance of personality and flawed humanity. Asuka is very physical in nature, directly assaulting several people and one innocent reinforced glass window over the course of the movies. She is a good candidate for the condition of touch. Mari, on the other hand, seems overly concerned with how Shinji smells, during 2.0 when they meet. She seems a great candidate for smell. This leaves us with Shinji, and his affinity for taste. A great deal is made about his gift for cooking in 2.0, and this has a profound effect on all the characters around him, providing him with something often missing in NGE: an outlet for him to relate to others. Housing a portion of Adam could also be connected the ‘curse of the Eva’, as the Adam fragment may be exerting a degree of control over its vessel’s AT field, and therefore physical form. In the case of Rei and Kaworu, those fragments are the sole inhabitants of their vessels, so as seeds of life they exercise control of their physical forms via their AT Fields. In Shinji's case, he got turned to goop within Eva-01's core for 14 years, until WILLE retrieves him; his soul seems to have determined his form be the same as that of his last memory. On a side note, I believe that Asuka and Mari are sisters (same mother, different fathers). The woman from the photo shown to Shinji by Fuyutski is a good candidate to be their mother, the equivalent of Kyoko from NGE. I believe that Gendo is a good candidate for her father, given her intimate knowledge of Nerv and motive to stop Nerv, as well as physical properties such as red hair and glasses. If we assume that at least portion of Kyoko resides within Eva-Unit 02, it goes a long way to explaining how Mari was able to pilot Eva-02 in the battle against Zeruel in 2.0, without any complicated subplot about a reconfigured core coming into play, with the core being inserted simply being Unit-02’s regular core. Conclusion. Bit of a brain dump there! I apologize in advance if any of this speculation has been raised elsewhere and my trawling has missed it. My intention was to get down what has been bouncing around in my head since seeing the second two films again! Please note that this speculation draws upon a) Some rules established by NGE b) The Classified Information c) The Continuity theory d) A fair bit of home-grown (but hopefully somewhat informed) speculation Thanks for reading, and in the hope that I haven’t just alienated the entire community, I eagerly look forward to hearing some thoughts on my thoughts! Asavarii.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Aug 16, 2014 2:53 pm One major problem: The FAR created the Seeds of Life in the first place because they were going extinct. To the point of possibly loading the Seeds with their souls to use for terraforming. By the time of mankind's birth, the FAR are probably totally gone.

[]:             Quote Post by Asavarii » Sat Aug 16, 2014 6:25 pm This occurred to me when I read the wiki entry for the FAR. I couldn't find where they cited that the FAR were extinct, only that their planet was under threat. Is there a source I'm not aware of? These are gods, by Eva's definition, after all. Extinct...?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Aug 17, 2014 6:54 am Asavarii wrote: This occurred to me when I read the wiki entry for the FAR. I couldn't find where they cited that the FAR were extinct, only that their planet was under threat. Is there a source I'm not aware of? These are gods, by Eva's definition, after all. Extinct...?
